كان: কানা (kana) means blind in Bengali — picture the blind man who kāna (WAS) forever walking into doors. Same sound; it means was.
قال: He CALLED out — qāla means he said. call ≈ qāl.
رب: A RABBI means my lord/master — ar-Rabb = the Lord. Same Semitic root: rabb ≈ rabbi.
يوم: Every DAY (yawm) you wake up and YAWN a giant yawn. yawm ≈ yawn. Sound-alike trap: قَوْم (qawm) = people — the কওমী one, with ق.
رسول: You already know it: রসূল (rôsul) in Bengali IS the Messenger. Exact same word.
بيت: You lay BAIT in a mouse-trap inside the house. bayt ≈ bait.
مسجد: মসজিদ (môsjid) — you know it in Bengali; and English mosque comes straight from masjid.
اذان: আজান (azan) — the very same word you already say for the call to prayer.
جدة: Grandma just booked a flight to JEDDAH — jadda = Jeddah, identical sound.
والد: Dad insists his word is always VALID, argument over. wālid ≈ valid.
حمام: A HAMMAM is a Turkish steam-bath — now picture a giant HAM steaming in the bathroom.
نظارة: Glasses sharpen your নজর (nôjor, Bengali for gaze/sight). Same root n-ẓ-r.
عظيم: The ʿaẓīm (great) champion is held in the highest ESTEEM. azeem ≈ esteem.
رحيم: Ar-Raheem, the Most Merciful — the common name রহিম (Rahim) literally means merciful.
قلب: When your heart (qalb) skips a beat you go GULP. qalb ≈ gulp.
كتاب: কিতাব (kitab) — you know it in Bengali: a (holy) book. kitāb = book.
علم: এলেম (elem) — Bengali for learning/knowledge. ʿilm = knowledge, same word.
ماء: Bleat MAAA! like a parched sheep desperate for water. māʾ = water.
سلام: সালাম (salam) — the greeting you already say. salām = peace.
اكبر: Allahu AKBAR — akbar = greatest; think Akbar the Great, the Mughal emperor.
شكرا: শুকরিয়া (shukriya) — Bengali/Urdu for thanks. shukran = thank you, same root.
كتب: কিতাব (kitab, book) comes from this — you WRITE (kataba) to make a book. Root k-t-b.
معلمة: A teacher gives you এলেম / ʿilm (knowledge): muʿallima (teacher) is from the same root ʿ-l-m.
ان: You already say it: إِنَّا لِلّٰهِ — ইন্না লিল্লাহি. That heavy doubled-ن inna is إِنَّ: it stamps what follows as CERTAIN — indeed. Don't confuse its skinny cousins: أَنْ (an) = 'that', إِنْ (in) = 'if', الآن (al-ān) = 'now'. Heavy nna = certainty.
ا: লা ইলাহা ইল্লাল্লাহ — lā ilāha ILLĀ-llāh: 'no god EXCEPT Allah'. The illā you say every day is إِلَّا = except. When recitation says إِلَّا, a hole is being cut: everything… except this.
دنيا: দুনিয়া is already your word — dunyā = this world. Exact same.
اخرة: আখেরাত — the Hereafter. Bengali kept the whole word: ākhira.
جنة: জান্নাত IS janna — Paradise. You've said it all your life.
نبي: নবী = nabiyy — prophet. Zero work needed.
قيامة: কেয়ামত — qiyāma, the Resurrection. Same word.
دين: The Bengali pair দ্বীন-দুনিয়া: dīn = religion, dunyā = world. yawm ad-dīn = the Day the dīn is settled.
حق: হক কথা = true talk — ḥaqq = truth, right. Same word.
صبر: সবর করো! — ṣabr. ṣabara = he was patient.
مال: মাল, মালামাল — māl = wealth, goods. Same word.
موت: হায়াত-মউত আল্লাহর হাতে — mawt = death (and ḥayāh = life).
حياة: হায়াত — lifespan. ḥayāh = life. (The set phrase: হায়াত-মউত.)
دعا: দোয়া — duʿāʾ. daʿā = he called upon (Allah).
سال: সওয়াল-জবাব (Q&A) — suʾāl = question, saʾala = he asked.
رحمة: আল্লাহর রহমত — raḥma = mercy. Same word; raḥīm is its adjective.
قوم: কওমী মাদ্রাসা = the people's madrasa — qawm = people, nation. Don't hear it as يَوْم (yawm, day — the YAWN one): ق q-awm = কওম people, ي y-awm = yawn day.
نفس: নফসের বিরুদ্ধে লড়াই — nafs = the self, the soul. Same word.
اهل: আহলে হাদীস, আহলে বাইত — ahl = the people of. Same word.
عمل: আমলনামা = the book of deeds — ʿamal = deed, ʿamila = he did.
عباد: ʿibād = (His) servants — the ones doing এবাদত. The name আবেদ (ʿābid) = worshipper.
ذكر: জিকির — dhikr. dhakara = he remembered, mentioned.
سجد: সিজদা — sajda. sajada = he prostrated. (মসজিদ = the place you do it.)
تاب: তওবা! তওবা! — tawba (repentance). tāba = he repented.
وعد: ওয়াদা করেছি — waʿd (promise). waʿada = he promised.
قتل: কতল করা — qatl. qatala = he killed.
دخل: দখল করা — to move in and take over. dakhala = he entered.
اخرج: মামলা খারিজ! — thrown OUT. akhraja = he brought out, expelled.
مخرج: মাখরাজ (tajweed: where the sound COMES OUT) — makhraj = exit.
رزق: রিজিক — rizq = provision. razaqa = he provided.
انزل: কুরআন নাজিল হলো — sent DOWN. anzala = he sent down.
غفر: আস্তাগফিরুল্লাহ + the name গফুর — the gh-f-r root. ghafara = he forgave.
غفور: গফুর — al-Ghafūr, the Oft-Forgiving. Same name you grew up hearing.
خلق: মাখলুক (creatures) + the name খালেক (al-Khāliq, the Creator) — khalaqa = he created.
حمد: মুহাম্মদ ﷺ literally means 'the much-PRAISED' — same root as the ḥamd in আলহামদুলিল্লাহ.
نور: নূর — light. Watch the vowel: nūr glows, nār burns.
نار: Flip নূর's vowel: nār = FIRE. নূর glows, nār burns.
ليل: লাইলি-মজনু: her name Laylā literally means NIGHT — layl.
نهار: নূরুন নাহার = 'light of the DAY' — nahār = daytime. Half of Bengal carries this word as a name.
سماء: Hear it inside আ-সমা-ন (asman = sky): samāʾ = sky.
ارض: arḍ ≈ EARTH — same meaning, nearly the same sound.
اصحاب: সাহাবা — the Companions. aṣḥāb = companions, people of. (আসহাবে কাহফ!)
مؤمنون: মুমিন — muʾmin, believer. muʾminūn = the believers.
صالحات: The name সালেহা = righteous woman — ṣāliḥāt = righteous deeds.
يعقلون: আক্কেল (আক্কেল দাঁত = wisdom tooth!) — ʿaql = reason. yaʿqilūn = they use their আক্কেল.
خالدون: খালেদ, খালিদ — the name means 'eternal'. khālidūn = abiding forever.
عليم: আলেম — ʿālim, one who KNOWS. ʿalīm = All-Knowing.
حكيم: হেকিম সাহেব — the wise healer. ḥakīm = All-Wise.
عزيز: আজিজ — ʿazīz = mighty, precious. al-ʿAzīz = the Almighty.
قدير: আব্দুল কাদের — al-Qadīr, the All-Powerful. qadīr = able over all things.
نصر: নাসির — the one who helps to victory. naṣara = he helped (to victory).
فضل: ফজিলত (merit) and ফজলুল হক — faḍl = bounty, grace.
خير: খয়রাত (alms — good works) — khayrāt. khayr = good, better.
كثير: Tafsir Ibn KATHĪR — kathīr = many, much. (He certainly wrote MUCH.)
بعد: বাদ মাগরিব, বাদ জুমা = AFTER maghrib — Bengali বাদ IS baʿd. baʿda = after.
كلام: আবুল কালাম = 'father of SPEECH'; আল্লাহর কালাম — kalām = speech.
شاء: মা শা আল্লাহ = 'what Allah WILLED' — shāʾa = he willed. You already say it.
ضرب: A cricket DRUBBING — ḍaraba = he struck. drub ≈ ḍarab.
ذهب: dhahaba = he WENT, dhahab = GOLD. He went for gold — one root, two words.
اذهب: idhhab = GO! (From dhahaba, he went — he went for GOLD, dhahab.)
مريض: So sick he groans মরি! মরি! — marīḍ = sick.
حساب: হিসাব-নিকাশ — ḥisāb = the account, the bill. yawm al-ḥisāb = Day of Reckoning.
تمر: TAMARind = tamr hindī, 'Indian DATE' — তেঁতুল's English name is secretly Arabic. tamr = dates.
لحم: BethLEHEM = Bayt Laḥm, 'House of MEAT' — laḥm = meat.
فندق: A FUN DUCK floats in the hotel pool — funduq = hotel.
باب: Ali Baba shouting 'iftaḥ yā simsim!' at the magic DOOR — bāb = door, gate.
يمين: বিনইয়ামীন (Binyamin, Yusuf's brother) = 'son of the RIGHT hand' — yamīn = right.
جواز سفري: সফর, মুসাফির — safar = journey. jawāz safar = travel-pass = passport.
واحد: আব্দুল ওয়াহেদ — al-Wāḥid, the One. wāḥid = one.
صديق: আবু বকর সিদ্দীক, the truthful — your ṣadīq (friend) is the one who's সিদ্দিক with you. Same truth-root.
اعرف: আরিফ = the one who KNOWS — aʿrifu = I know.
ابن: ইবনে বতুতা, ইবনে সিনা — ibn = son (of).
لكن: লেকিন — the Urdu-Hindi 'but' you know from films came straight from Arabic lākin.
ممكن: না-মুমকিন (impossible) from Hindi films — mumkin = possible.
يعني: Urdu-Hindi ইয়ানি ('that is…') — yaʿnī = meaning… Same filler word.
طيب: Recep TAYYIP Erdoğan — ṭayyib = good, fine, okay.
حار بارد: bārid = BRRR-id, the cold one. So the other one, ḥārr, is hot.
توضا: অজু — wuḍūʾ. tawaḍḍaʾa = he did অজু. Same root.
انظر: নজর দাও! — unẓur = look! (Same নজর as your glasses hook.)
اذا: إِذَا جَاءَ نَصْرُ اللّٰهِ — 'WHEN the help of Allah comes' (Surah An-Nasr — you recite it). idhā = when/if. Not هٰذَا (hādhā = THIS) — that one starts with HA: 'HA! THIS one!'
